top_signals.title: Goldman Sachs Sounds Alarm Over Fresh Threat to Global Oil Supplies
top_signals.summary: A week after it warned that an oil glut is coming, Goldman Sachs has done a U-turn, warning that the renewed hostilities in the Persian Gulf threaten an extended supply disruption. “While Middle Eastern producers have started reopening their shut-in wells over the last month, Hormuz disruptions could slow down the production recovery,” the bank’s commodity analysts said, as quoted by Bloomberg. They added that Middle East oil production remains 10.5 million barrels daily below pre-war levels. “The recent attacks on tankers…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Aston Martin’s Creditors Band Together Amid Debt Rout
top_signals.summary: A group of creditors to Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings Plc has signed a cooperation pact as mounting losses raise concerns among bondholders.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
